title: unciferia wagneri
intro: Unciferia wagneri, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus or Ma'o, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used as a natural dye due to its vibrant red color.
